name: 4p16_3_Wolf_Hirschhorn_breakpoints
description: 4p16.3 Wolf-Hirschhorn syndrome (WHS) terminal-deletion region. The 4p subtelomere harbours olfactory-receptor (OR), zinc-finger and FAM149A paralog SD blocks that mediate non-recurrent terminal 4p deletions of variable size. WHSC1/NSD2 and WHSC2 are the dosage-critical genes; the 165-kb WHSCR-2 minimal region lies ~1.9 Mb from the telomere.

architecture.internal_structure: Subtelomeric SD-rich mosaic of OR clusters (OR4F-like), ZNF141/ZNF721 paralogs and FAM193A-flanking duplicons at 92-97% identity; unique core contains LETM1, WHSC1/NSD2, WHSC2/NELFA, MSX1.
